mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near the zero lower bound (0.19%), the persistent gap between policy rate and equity earnings yield compresses the equity risk premium and sustains a positive drift in S&P 500 prices as investors search for yield in risk assets. This mechanism is always-on while rates remain near zero.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.19% as of 2010-10, near zero lower bound
regime_hint: zero interest rate policy / QE era
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns during sub-0.5% FEDFUNDS regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in UNRATE exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals an inflection in labor market slack that is interpreted by equity markets as evidence of self-sustaining recovery, triggering a positive repricing of forward earnings expectations over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.6% as of 2010-10, elevated post-crisis level
failure_condition: In triggered instances, 20-day forward SPX returns are negative or indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05) across at least 60% of occurrences in a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) reflects a broad-based consumer and housing demand recovery that is not captured by either series alone; simultaneous positive growth in both signals a durable demand-side expansion that lifts forward S&P 500 returns over the next month, whereas divergence between the two series produces no reliable signal.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 373103M and HOUST at 610K units as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint RSAFS-HOUST co-movement signal is not statistically greater than the average of the two single-series Rank ICs, or falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession industrial recovery regime, sustained month-over-month increases in INDPRO reflect rebuilding of production capacity and inventory restocking, which forward equity markets price positively as corporate earnings expectations rise over the following month. This effect is expected to be strongest specifically during the early recovery phase and to weaken or reverse in late-cycle regimes with capacity constraints.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 93.19 (2017=100) as of latest snapshot, below pre-crisis levels indicating recovery headroom
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum fails to produce statistically significant positive 20-day forward returns (p > 0.05) during the identified recovery regime, or the sign of the relationship flips within the same regime across two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation above 2.5% while FEDFUNDS remains near zero signals rising inflation-adjusted policy accommodation risk, which markets interpret as a precursor to future tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as discount rate expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY inflation exceeds 2.5% while FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 218.372 and FEDFUNDS at 0.19% as of latest snapshot
regime_hint: low-rate regime with rising inflation pressure
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following triggered inflation breaches are not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or occur less than 40% of the time in triggered instances over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in PAYEMS (nonfarm payrolls) reflect improving corporate hiring intentions and near-term consumer income growth, producing a short-lived positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets react to the incremental employment print. This is a continuous, always-on trend signal not requiring threshold triggers.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130462 thousand persons as of 2010-10
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month changes (Rank IC < 0.03) over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a fragile post-crisis recovery regime with high revision risk in GDPC1, sustained quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth above 2% annualized signals confirmed economic expansion, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter; however, in a stagnation or double-dip regime, the same signal loses predictive power due to data revision noise.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13260.7 billion chained dollars as of 2010-Q2, indicating gradual post-crisis recovery
regime_hint: post-crisis GDP recovery confirmed by consecutive positive quarters
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following positive GDPC1 growth prints are not significantly higher than the unconditional baseline, or the relationship is dominated by subsequent downward data revisions in more than 50% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of near-zero FEDFUNDS and elevated UNRATE above 8% indicates a policy-labor slack mismatch consistent with an extended low-rate regime (Taylor-rule implied undershooting), which markets price as a durable tailwind for equity valuations over the following quarter; the effect requires both conditions jointly, since either alone is insufficient to indicate policy commitment credibility.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.19% and UNRATE at 9.6% as of latest snapshot
regime_hint: extended zero-rate policy amid high unemployment
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns during joint FEDFUNDS<0.5%/UNRATE>8% conditions are not significantly different from unconditional baseline returns, or Rank IC of the joint signal is not greater than either single-series signal over a 3-year walk-forward window.
